# Created diverging bar charts of CEL (Condición Étnico-Lingüística) distribution by urban/rural and by age group, using ggplot_build to correctly position segment labels, with left- and right-side population totals.

## User

After count-plot-editorial, create a new version of the plot, but not sorted by maternal language but rather by age blocks, group in ranges of fifteen years (0-14, 15-29, 30-44, etc.).

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01JEjY5y7Y9Yr1RVhxaBJefQ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 56.8KB, 1422 lines, showing lines 881-1080 (truncated)]\n   881→                    ...[truncated, total 9227 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 56.8KB, 1422 lines, showing lines 881-1080 (truncated)]\n                             levels = y_labs_ed))\n\nbs <- 14\n\nggplot(div_df, aes(x = n_plot, y = idioma_grp, fill = outcome_plot)) +\n  geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = FALSE) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = seg_inside,\n    aes(x = mid, y = idioma_grp, label = label, color = text_col),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.26, fontface = \"bold\"\n  ) +\n  scale_color_identity() +\n  geom_text_repel(\n    data = seg_above,\n    aes(x = mid, y = idioma_grp, label = label),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.21, fontface = \"bold\",\n    color = \"grey30\",\n    direction = \"x\", nudge_y = 0.44,\n    segment.size = 0.2, segment.color = \"grey60\",\n    min.segment.length = 0,\n    box.padding = 0.15, force = 3, max.overlaps = 20\n  ) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = right_totals_ed,\n    aes(x = right_total * 1.02, y = idioma_grp,\n        label = scales::comma(right_total)),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 0, size = bs * 0.26, fontface = \"bold\",\n    color = \"grey30\"\n  ) +\n  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1.2, color = \"grey20\") +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = -2500000, y = 5.7,\n           label = \"\\u2190  Does not identify / No response\",\n           size = bs * 0.26, fontface = \"bold\", color = \"#4a5568\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = 1500000, y = 5.7,\n           label = \"Identifies as indigenous  \\u2192\",\n           size = bs * 0.26, fontface = \"bold\", color = \"#b35806\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  scale_x_continuous(\n    labels = function(x) scales::comma(abs(x)),\n    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.08, 0.14))\n  ) +\n  scale_fill_manual(values = outcome_colors) +\n  coord_cartesian(clip = \"off\", ylim = c(0.4, 5.5)) +\n  labs(\n    title    = \"Mother tongue and indigenous identity \\u2014 CNPV 2024\",\n    subtitle = \"idioma_mat vs p32_pueblos  |  n = 11,365,333\",\n    x = NULL, y = NULL\n  ) +\n  theme_void(base_size = bs) +\n  theme(\n    plot.title    = element_text(face = \"bold\", size = rel(1.35), margin = margin(b = 3)),\n    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = \"grey40\", size = rel(0.8), margin = margin(b = 16)),\n    plot.margin   = margin(20, 30, 10, 10),\n    axis.text.y   = element_text(hjust = 1, color = \"grey20\",\n                                 size = rel(0.85), margin = margin(r = 8)),\n    axis.text.x   = element_text(color = \"grey50\", size = rel(0.6), margin = margin(t = 5))\n  )\n```\n\n------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n## Age, language shift, and indigenous identity\n\nA key question is whether younger generations are less likely to both speak an indigenous language *and* self-identify as indigenous. The full census allows us to examine this cleanly, again via lazy aggregation.\n\n```{r}\n#| label: age-cross-collect\n#| cache: true\nage_cross <- ds |>\n  filter(!is.na(p26_edad), !is.na(idioma_mat), !is.na(p32_pueblos)) |>\n  mutate(\n    age_group = case_when(\n      p26_edad <  15 ~ \"0–14\",\n      p26_edad <  30 ~ \"15–29\",\n      p26_edad <  45 ~ \"30–44\",\n      p26_edad <  60 ~ \"45–59\",\n      TRUE           ~ \"60+\"\n    )\n  ) |>\n  group_by(idioma_mat, p32_pueblos, age_group) |>\n  summarise(n = n(), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  collect() |>\n  mutate(\n    idioma_label  = idioma_cats$label[match(idioma_mat, idioma_cats$code)],\n    pueblos_label = pueblos_cats$label[match(p32_pueblos, pueblos_cats$code)],\n    age_group     = factor(age_group, levels = c(\"0–14\",\"15–29\",\"30–44\",\"45–59\",\"60+\"))\n  )\n```\n\n### Self-identification rate by age\n\nThe share of the population that self-identifies as indigenous declines markedly in younger cohorts, from 49% of 60+ year-olds to 28% of 0–14 year-olds.\n\n```{r}\n#| label: id-rate-by-age\ntotal_by_age <- age_cross |>\n  group_by(age_group) |>\n  summarise(total_pop = sum(n), .groups = \"drop\")\n\nidentifies_by_age <- age_cross |>\n  filter(!pueblos_label %in% c(\"No se autoidentifica\", \"Sin respuesta\"),\n         !is.na(pueblos_label)) |>\n  group_by(age_group) |>\n  summarise(n_identifies = sum(n), .groups = \"drop\")\n\ntotal_by_age |>\n  left_join(identifies_by_age, by = \"age_group\") |>\n  mutate(pct_identifies = round(100 * n_identifies / total_pop, 1))\n```\n\n### Mother tongue of self-identifiers, by age\n\nAmong those who *do* self-identify, the proportion who also speak an indigenous mother tongue drops sharply with youth: from 76% in the 60+ cohort to just 22% in the 0–14 cohort. The inverse — self-identifying as indigenous while having Castellano as mother tongue — rises from 24% among elders to 72% among children.\n\n```{r}\n#| label: lang-by-age\nindigenous_identifies <- age_cross |>\n  filter(!pueblos_label %in% c(\"No se autoidentifica\", \"Sin respuesta\"),\n         !is.na(pueblos_label)) |>\n  mutate(\n    lang_group = case_when(\n      idioma_label == \"Castellano\" ~ \"Castellano\",\n      !is.na(idioma_label) & idioma_label != \"No habla\" ~ \"Indigenous language\",\n      TRUE ~ \"Other/unknown\"\n    )\n  ) |>\n  group_by(age_group, lang_group) |>\n  summarise(n = sum(n), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  group_by(age_group) |>\n  mutate(total = sum(n), pct = round(100 * n / total, 1)) |>\n  ungroup()\n\nindigenous_identifies |>\n  filter(lang_group %in% c(\"Castellano\", \"Indigenous language\")) |>\n  select(age_group, lang_group, n, pct)\n```\n\n```{r}\n#| label: age-language-plot\n#| fig-height: 6\nlibrary(patchwork)\n\np_rate <- total_by_age |>\n  left_join(identifies_by_age, by = \"age_group\") |>\n  mutate(pct = n_identifies / total_pop) |>\n  ggplot(aes(x = age_group, y = pct, group = 1)) +\n  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +\n  geom_point(size = 3) +\n  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(0, 0.55)) +\n  labs(\n    title = \"A. Indigenous self-identification rate by age group\",\n    x = \"Age group\", y = \"% of age group\"\n  ) +\n  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)\n\np_lang <- indigenous_identifies |>\n  filter(lang_group %in% c(\"Castellano\", \"Indigenous language\")) |>\n  mutate(\n    lang_group = factor(lang_group, levels = c(\"Indigenous language\", \"Castellano\")),\n    pct_val    = pct / 100\n  ) |>\n  ggplot(aes(x = age_group, y = pct_val, fill = lang_group)) +\n  geom_col() +\n  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +\n  scale_fill_manual(\n    values = c(\"Indigenous language\" = \"#2166ac\", \"Castellano\" = \"#d73027\"),\n    name = \"Mother tongue\"\n  ) +\n  labs(\n    title = \"B. Mother tongue of indigenous self-identifiers, by age\",\n    x = \"Age group\", y = \"% of self-identifiers\"\n  ) +\n  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +\n  theme(legend.position = \"bottom\")\n\np_rate / p_lang\n```\n\nThese two panels together capture a generational divergence: identity is partially decoupling from language. The 0–14 cohort has the lowest self-identification rate of any age group AND, among those who do identify, the highest share with Castellano as mother tongue. This is consistent with ongoing language shift — Spanish replacing indigenous languages across generations — while ethnic identity persists, albeit at lower rates, among some families.\n\nA caveat: the 0–14 cohort's responses are partially proxy-reported by parents, which may introduce different patterns from adult self-reporting.\n\n------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n## Daily language use vs mother tongue (idioma_mayor_uso × idioma_mat)\n\n`idioma_mayor_uso` records the language used most frequently in daily life. Cross-referencing it against `idioma_mat` (mother tongue) reveals whether people who *learned* an indigenous language still *use* it, or have shifted to Spanish in daily practice.\n\n```{r}\n#| label: lang-use-collect\n#| cache: true\nlang_use <- ds |>\n  filter(!is.na(idioma_mat), !is.na(idioma_mayor_uso)) |>\n  group_by(idioma_mat, idioma_mayor_uso) |>\n  summarise(n = n(), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  collect() |>\n  mutate(\n    mat_label = idioma_cats$label[match(idioma_mat,       idioma_cats$code)],\n    uso_label = idioma_cats$label[match(idioma_mayor_uso, idioma_cats$code)]\n  )\n"},"elapsedMs":82}</script></tool-result></div>

idioma_grp)\n\n  df <- df |>\n    mutate(idioma_grp = factor(y_labels[as.character(idioma_grp)],\n                               levels = y_labels))\n\n  right_totals <- right_totals |>\n    mutate(idioma_grp = factor(y_labels[as.character(idioma_grp)],\n                               levels = y_labels))\n  seg_labs <- seg_labs |>\n    mutate(idioma_grp = right_totals,\n      aes(x = right_total * 1.02, y = idioma_grp,\n          label = scales::comma(right_total)),\n      inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 0, size = total_label_size, fontface = \"bold\"\n    ) +\n    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4) +\n    scale_x_continuous(\n      labels = function(x) scales::comma(abs(x)),\n      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.10, 0.15))\n    ) +\n \", arrow_left,\n                        \"          \", arrow_right, \" \\u2192\"),\n      x = x_label, y = NULL, fill = NULL\n    ) +\n    theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) +\n    theme(\n      legend.position  = \"bottom\",\n      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()\n    )\n}\n```\n\n#### English (counts)\n\n```{r}\n#| label: count-plot-en\n#| fig-width: 10\n#| fig-height: 5.5\nmake_count_diverging(\n  make_count_diverging_df(en_map, \"Other indigenous language\", lang_order),\n  lvls_right  = c(\"Generic identity\", \"Different group\",\n                   \"Identifies as indigenous\", \"Matching group\"),\n  lvls_left   = c(\"No response\", \"Does not identify\"),\n  cols        = outcome_colors,\n  title       = \"Mother tongue and indigenous identity — CNPV 2024 (counts)\",\n  subtitle    = \"idioma_mat vs p32_pueblos | n = 11,365,333\",\n  x_label     = \"Number of persons\",\n  arrow_left  = \"Does not identify / No response\",\n  arrow_right = \"Identifies as indigenous\"\n)\n```\n\n#### Spanish / Español (conteos)\n\n```{r}\n#| label: count-plot-es\n#| fig-width: 10\n#| fig-height: 5.5\nmake_count_diverging(\n  make_count_diverging_df(es_map, \"Otro idioma nativo\", lang_order_es),\n  lvls_right  = c(\"Identidad genérica\", \"Grupo diferente\",\n                   \"Se autoidentifica como indígena\", \"Grupo coincidente\"),\n  lvls_left   = c(\"Sin respuesta\", \"No se autoidentifica\"),\n  cols        = outcome_colors_es,\n  title       = \"Lengua materna e identidad indígena — CNPV 2024 (conteos)\",\n  subtitle    = \"idioma_mat vs p32_pueblos | n = 11.365.333\",\n  x_label     = \"Número de personas\",\n  arrow_left  = \"No se autoidentifica / Sin respuesta\",\n  arrow_right = \"Se autoidentifica como indígena\"\n)\n``` \n\n#### Editorial version (English)\n\n```{r}\n#| label: count-plot-editorial\n#| fig-width: 12\n#| fig-height: 5.5\ndiv_df <- make_count_diverging_df(en_map, \"Other indigenous language\", lang_order) |>\n  mutate(outcome_plot = factor(outcome_plot, levels = c(\n    \"Generic identity\", \"Different group\", \"Identifies as indigenous\", \"Matching group\",\n    \"No response\", \"Does not identify\"\n  )))\n\nx_range_div <- max(abs(div_df$n_plot))\n\n# Segment labels — 5% threshold keeps it clean\nseg_labs_ed <- div_df |>\n  arrange(idioma_grp, side, desc(outcome_plot)) |>\n  group_by(idioma_grp, side) |>\n  mutate(\n    abs_cum   = cumsum(n),\n    abs_start = abs_cum - n,\n    mid       = if_else(side == \"left\",\n                        -(abs_start + abs_cum) / 2,\n                         (abs_start + abs_cum) / 2)\n  ) |>\n  ungroup() |>\n  filter(n > 20000, n / total > 0.05, n / total < 0.85) |>\n  mutate(\n    label    = label_compact(n),\n    text_col = if_else(\n      outcome_plot %in% c(\"Matching group\", \"Does not identify\",\n                          \"Identifies as indigenous\"),\n      \"white\", \"grey20\"\n    )\n  )\n\nseg_inside <- seg_labs_ed |> filter(n > x_range_div * 0.05)\nseg_above  <- seg_labs_ed |> filter(n <= x_range_div * 0.05)\n\nright_totals_ed <- div_df |>\n  filter(side == \"right\") |>\n  group_by(idioma_grp) |>\n  summarise(right_total = sum(n), .groups = \"drop\")\n\n# Y-axis labels: bold name + non-bold total on second line\ny_labs_ed <- div_df |>\n  group_by(idioma_grp) |>\n  summarise(total = first(total), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  mutate(y_lab = paste0(idioma_grp, \"\\nn = \", scales::comma(total))) |>\n  pull(y_lab, name = idioma_grp)\n\n# Remap factor levels for all data frames\ndiv_df <- div_df |>\n  mutate(idioma_grp = factor(y_labs_ed[as.character(idioma_grp)],\n                             levels = y_labs_ed))\nseg_inside <- seg_inside |>\n  mutate(idioma_grp = factor(y_labs_ed[as.character(idioma_grp)],\n                             levels = y_labs_ed))\nseg_above <- seg_above |>\n  mutate(idioma_grp = factor(y_labs_ed[as.character(idioma_grp)],\n                             levels = y_labs_ed))\nright_totals_ed <- right_totals_ed |>\n  mutate(idioma_grp = factor(y_labs_ed[as.character(idioma_grp)],\n                             levels = y_labs_ed))\n\nbs <- 14\n\nggplot(div_df, aes(x = n_plot, y = idioma_grp, fill = outcome_plot)) +\n  geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = FALSE) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = seg_inside,\n    aes(x = mid, y = idioma_grp, label = label, color = text_col),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.26, fontface = \"bold\""},"elapsedMs":74}</script></tool-result></div>

\"#bababa\",\n  \"Sin datos de identidad\"          = \"#f5f5f5\"\n)\nes_map <- c(\n  \"Matching group\"           = \"Grupo coincidente\",\n  \"Different group\"          = \"Grupo diferente\",\n  \"Identifies as indigenous\" = \"Se autoidentifica como indígena\",\n  \"Generic identity\"         = \"Identidad genérica\",\n  \"Does not identify\"        = \"No se autoidentifica\",\n  \"No response\"              = \"Sin respuesta\",\n  \"No identity data\"         = \"Sin datos de identidad\"\n)\n\nmake_bar_plot(\n  make_plot_df(es_map),\n  lvls     = outcome_levels_es,\n  cols     = outcome_colors_es,\n  title    = \"Alineación entre lengua materna e identidad indígena — censo completo\",\n  subtitle = \"idioma_mat vs p32_pueblos | n = 11.365.333 | números: total de hablantes\",\n  x_label  = \"Proporción de hablantes\"\n)\n```\n\n------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n## Full-census language–identity alignment: counts (aggregated)\n\nThe same data as above, displayed as **population counts** rather than percentages, with all languages except Quechua, Aymara, Guaraní, and Castellano collapsed into \"Other indigenous language\" / \"Otro idioma nativo\".\n\n```{r}\n#| label: count-plot-setup\nlibrary(ggrepel)\n\nkeep_langs  <- c(\"Guaraní\", \"Aymara\", \"Quechua\", \"Castellano\")\nlang_order  <- c(\"Castellano\", \"Other indigenous language\", \"Guaraní\", \"Aymara\", \"Quechua\")\nlang_order_es <- c(\"Castellano\", \"Otro idioma nativo\", \"Guaraní\", \"Aymara\", \"Quechua\")\n\n# Categories by side\nright_cats <- c(\"Matching group\", \"Identifies as indigenous\", \"Different group\",\n                \"Generic identity\")\nleft_cats  <- c(\"Does not identify\", \"No response\")\n\ncount_df <- alignment_full2 |>\n  mutate(idioma_grp = if_else(idioma_label %in% keep_langs, idioma_label,\n                              \"Other indigenous language\")) |>\n  group_by(idioma_grp, outcome) |>\n  summarise(n = sum(n), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  group_by(idioma_grp) |>\n  mutate(total = sum(n)) |>\n  ungroup()\n\nmake_count_diverging_df <- function(outcome_map, other_lbl, lang_lvls) {\n  count_df |>\n    mutate(\n      outcome_plot = outcome_map[outcome],\n      # Castellano has no \"Matching group\"; remap \"Different group\" → identifies\n      outcome_plot = if_else(\n        idioma_grp == \"Castellano\" & outcome == \"Different group\",\n        outcome_map[[\"Identifies as indigenous\"]],\n        outcome_plot\n      ),\n      idioma_grp = if_else(idioma_grp == \"Other indigenous language\","},"elapsedMs":65}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_01M8ChKsKttkg3p2cs2vJkTJ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/wiki-graph/bolivia-censo-2024.qmd","offset":665,"limit":50},"caption":"bolivia-censo-2024.qmd:666-715"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01M8ChKsKttkg3p2cs2vJkTJ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 56.8KB, 1422 lines, showing lines 666-715 (truncated)]\n   666→                     ...[truncated, total 2253 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 56.8KB, 1422 lines, showing lines 666-715 (truncated)]\n                           other_lbl, idioma_grp)\n    ) |>\n    # Collapse duplicates from Castellano remapping\n    group_by(idioma_grp, outcome_plot, total) |>\n    summarise(n = sum(n), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n    mutate(\n      side   = if_else(outcome_plot %in% outcome_map[left_cats], \"left\", \"right\"),\n      n_plot = if_else(side == \"left\", -n, n),\n      idioma_grp = factor(idioma_grp, levels = lang_lvls)\n    )\n}\n\nlabel_compact <- function(x) {\n  case_when(\n    abs(x) >= 1e6 ~ paste0(round(abs(x) / 1e6, 1), \"M\"),\n    abs(x) >= 1e3 ~ paste0(round(abs(x) / 1e3, 1), \"K\"),\n    TRUE           ~ as.character(abs(x))\n  )\n}\n\nmake_count_diverging <- function(df, lvls_right, lvls_left, cols, title,\n                                 subtitle, x_label, arrow_left, arrow_right,\n                                 base_size = 11) {\n  # Font sizes scale with base_size\n  seg_label_size  <- base_size * 0.23\n  total_label_size <- base_size * 0.27\n\n  # Factor order: right categories (inner → outer), then left (inner → outer)\n  df <- df |>\n    mutate(outcome_plot = factor(outcome_plot, levels = c(lvls_right, lvls_left)))\n\n  # Compute label positions (desc: ggplot2 stacks last level closest to axis)\n  seg_labs <- df |>\n    arrange(idioma_grp, side, desc(outcome_plot)) |>\n    group_by(idioma_grp, side) |>\n    mutate(\n      abs_cum   = cumsum(n),\n      abs_start = abs_cum - n,\n      mid       = if_else(side == \"left\",\n                          -(abs_start + abs_cum) / 2,\n                           (abs_start + abs_cum) / 2)\n    ) |>\n    ungroup() |>\n    # Label segments > 20k, > 3% of total, and < 85% (dominant segments are obvious)\n    filter(n > 20000, n / total > 0.03, n / total < 0.85) |>\n    mutate(label = label_compact(n))\n\n  # Right-side total (identifies) for end-of-bar labels\n  right_totals <- df |>\n    filter(side == \"right\") |>"},"elapsedMs":51}</script></tool-result></div>

Now I need to understand the source data — `alignment_full2`. Let me also check what `age_cross` looks like since we'll need age groups.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_011XcGaUY2qdizDD7BwfnvAE"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"grep","input":{"pattern":"alignment_full2","path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/wiki-graph/bolivia-censo-2024.qmd"},"caption":"\"alignment_full2\" in bolivia-censo-2024.qmd","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"Pattern: alignment_full2\nPath: /Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/wiki-graph/bolivia-censo-2024.qmd"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_011XcGaUY2qdizDD7BwfnvAE"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"bolivia-censo-2024.qmd:453:alignment_full2 <- alignment_full\nbolivia-censo-2024.qmd:646:count_df <- alignment_full2 |>\n"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"bolivia-censo-2024.qmd:453:alignment_full2 <- alignment_full\nbolivia-censo-2024.qmd:646:count_df <- alignment_full2 |>\n","language":"text"},"elapsedMs":4}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_01S7tY4pgxYFocnEARAdueeo"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/wiki-graph/bolivia-censo-2024.qmd","offset":400,"limit":60},"caption":"bolivia-censo-2024.qmd:401-460"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01S7tY4pgxYFocnEARAdueeo"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 56.8KB, 1422 lines, showing lines 401-460 (truncated)]\n   401→\n   402→-------------...[truncated, total 2821 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 56.8KB, 1422 lines, showing lines 401-460 (truncated)]\n\n------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n## Full-census language–identity alignment: proportions and counts\n\nThe sample results above are based on 10,000 rows, leaving small groups with very few observations. The full 11.4M-person dataset is queried lazily via `arrow` — only the grouped counts are collected.\n\n```{r}\n#| label: full-cross-collect\n#| cache: true\nfull_cross <- ds |>\n  group_by(idioma_mat, p32_pueblos) |>\n  summarise(n = n(), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  collect()\n```\n\n```{r}\n#| label: full-alignment\nfull_cross_decoded <- full_cross |>\n  mutate(\n    idioma_label  = idioma_cats$label[match(idioma_mat, idioma_cats$code)],\n    pueblos_label = pueblos_cats$label[match(p32_pueblos, pueblos_cats$code)]\n  )\n\n# Kabineña→Cavineño and Zamuco→Ayoreo are naming convention differences (same group),\n# so they are treated as matching in the classifier.\nclassify_alignment2 <- function(idioma, pueblos) {\n  equiv <- c(\"Kabineña\" = \"Cavineño\", \"Zamuco\" = \"Ayoreo\", \"Bésiro\" = \"Chiquitano\")\n  expected <- coalesce(equiv[idioma], idioma)\n  case_when(\n    str_detect(pueblos, fixed(expected, ignore_case = TRUE)) |\n      str_detect(expected, fixed(pueblos, ignore_case = TRUE))  ~ \"Matching group\",\n    pueblos == \"No se autoidentifica\"                           ~ \"Does not identify\",\n    pueblos == \"Sin respuesta\"                                  ~ \"No response\",\n    pueblos == \"Otras declaraciones\"                            ~ \"Generic identity\",\n    !is.na(pueblos)                                             ~ \"Different group\",\n    TRUE                                                        ~ \"No identity data\"\n  )\n}\n\nlangs_with_castellano <- c(indigenous_langs, \"Castellano\")\n\nalignment_full <- full_cross_decoded |>\n  filter(!is.na(idioma_label), idioma_label %in% langs_with_castellano) |>\n  mutate(outcome = classify_alignment2(idioma_label, pueblos_label)) |>\n  group_by(idioma_label, outcome) |>\n  summarise(n = sum(n), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  group_by(idioma_label) |>\n  mutate(total = sum(n), pct = round(100 * n / total, 1)) |>\n  ungroup()\n\n# Alias used in the count-plot chunks below\nalignment_full2 <- alignment_full\n\nalignment_full |>\n  select(idioma_label, outcome, pct, total) |>\n  pivot_wider(names_from = outcome, values_from = pct, values_fill = 0) |>\n  arrange(desc(total))\n```\n"},"elapsedMs":60}</script></tool-result></div>

I'll need to query `ds` for the age × identity alignment data grouped by age_group, or check if I can reconstruct it from the available variables.

For the age version of the chart, I need to collapse across all languages and show age groups on the y-axis with identity alignment on the x-axis, similar to the original plot structure. Now I have a clear picture. I need to create the same diverging bar chart but with age groups on the y-axis instead of language groups. This requires a new aggregation from `ds` that includes age. Let me build it.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01SAYFtmXcMgFMziWXxhdrhJ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\n# Collect age × language × identity alignment\n# (age_cross may not be in session, so re-a...[truncated, total 527 chars]","intent":"Collect age × language × identity cross-tabulation from lazy census dataset"},"intent":"Collect age × language × identity cross-tabulation from lazy census dataset","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\n# Collect age × language × identity alignment\n# (age_cross may not be in session, so re-aggregate from ds)\nage_align_raw <- ds |>\n  filter(!is.na(p26_edad), !is.na(idioma_mat), !is.na(p32_pueblos)) |>\n  mutate(\n    age_group = case_when(\n      p26_edad <  15 ~ \"0-14\",\n      p26_edad <  30 ~ \"15-29\",\n      p26_edad <  45 ~ \"30-44\",\n      p26_edad <  60 ~ \"45-59\",\n      TRUE           ~ \"60+\"\n    )\n  ) |>\n  group_by(age_group, idioma_mat, p32_pueblos) |>\n  summarise(n = n(), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  collect()\n\nage_align_raw\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01SAYFtmXcMgFMziWXxhdrhJ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\n# A tibble: 2,154 × 4\n   age_group idioma_mat p32_pueblos       n\n   <chr>        ...[truncated, total 644 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# A tibble: 2,154 × 4\n   age_group idioma_mat p32_pueblos       n\n   <chr>          <int>       <int>   <int>\n 1 45-59              6          98  719459\n 2 45-59              2           3  164301\n 3 15-29              6          98 1762652\n 4 30-44              6          98 1285754\n 5 45-59              6           3  120064\n 6 45-59              2          57   21053\n 7 30-44              6           3  253304\n 8 30-44              2           3  143740\n 9 30-44              6          40  155603\n10 45-59              6          57   37560\n# ℹ 2,144 more rows\n# ℹ Use `print(n = ...)` to see more rows"},"elapsedMs":8526}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_01UiC3JXLxcSgNfPkWywWbiz"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\n# Decode and classify alignment, then aggregate by age group\nage_align <- age_align_raw |...[truncated, total 565 chars]","intent":"Classify language-identity alignment and aggregate by age group"},"intent":"Classify language-identity alignment and aggregate by age group","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\n# Decode and classify alignment, then aggregate by age group\nage_align <- age_align_raw |>\n  mutate(\n    idioma_label  = idioma_cats$label[match(idioma_mat, idioma_cats$code)],\n    pueblos_label = pueblos_cats$label[match(p32_pueblos, pueblos_cats$code)]\n  ) |>\n  filter(!is.na(idioma_label), idioma_label %in% langs_with_castellano) |>\n  mutate(outcome = classify_alignment2(idioma_label, pueblos_label)) |>\n  group_by(age_group, outcome) |>\n  summarise(n = sum(n), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  group_by(age_group) |>\n  mutate(total = sum(n)) |>\n  ungroup()\n\nage_align\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

731-790 (truncated)]\n   731→  ) +\n   732→  theme_...[truncated, total 2087 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 38.8KB, 1140 lines, showing lines 731-790 (truncated)]\n  ) +\n  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)\n```\n\n### CEL by urban/rural\n\n```{r}\n#| label: cel-urban-rural\n#| fig-height: 6\nurbrur_labels <- c(\"1\" = \"Urban\", \"2\" = \"Rural\")\n\nalbo_vars |>\n  mutate(urbrur_label = urbrur_labels[as.character(urbrur)]) |>\n  filter(!is.na(urbrur_label)) |>\n  count(urbrur_label, cel) |>\n  group_by(urbrur_label) |>\n  mutate(\n    pct     = n / sum(n),\n geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +\n  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pct * 100, 1), \"%\")),\n            hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +\n  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(),\n                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.16))) +\n  scale_fill_manual(values = cel_colors) +\n  facet_wrap(~ urbrur_label) +\n  labs(\n    title    = \"CEL by urban/rural\",\n    subtitle = \"Bolivia CPV 2024\",\n    x = \"Share of population\", y = NULL\n  ) +\n  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +\n  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = \"bold\"))\n```\n\n### CEL by department\n\n```{r}\n#| label: cel-dept-total\n#| fig-height: 7\ndept_labels <- c(\n  \"1\" = \"Chuquisaca\", \"2\" = \"La Paz\",    \"3\" = \"Cochabamba\",\n  \"4\" = \"Oruro\",      \"5\" = \"Potosí\",    \"6\" = \"Tarija\",\n  \"7\" = \"Santa Cruz\", \"8\" = \"Beni\",      \"9\" = \"Pando\"\n)\n\nalbo_vars |>\n  mutate(dept = dept_labels[as.character(idep)]) |>\n  count(dept, cel) |>\n  group_by(dept) |>\n  mutate(\n    pct     = n / sum(n),\n    cel_chr = as.character(cel),\n    dept    = fct_reorder(dept, pct * (cel %in% c(6, 7)), sum)\n  ) |>"},"elapsedMs":58}</script></tool-result></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01T2EAoJYHigiefwWPWhwiSY"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 38.8KB, 1140 lines, showing lines 681-710 (truncated)]\n   681→### CEL distribution\n...[truncated, total 1126 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 38.8KB, 1140 lines, showing lines 681-710 (truncated)]\n### CEL distribution\n\n```{r}\n#| label: cel-plot\n#| fig-height: 5\ncel_labels <- c(\n  \"0\"   = \"0 — No identity, no language\",\n  \"1\"   = \"1 — No identity, speaks (no childhood)\",\n  \"1.5\" = \"1.5 — No identity, childhood language only\",\n  \"2\"   = \"2 — No identity, speaks + childhood (bilingual)\",\n  \"3\"   = \"3 — No identity, speaks + childhood (monolingual)\",\n  \"4\"   = \"4 — Identity only\",\n  \"4.5\" = \"4.5 — Identity + childhood (no current language)\",\n  \"5\"   = \"5 — Identity + language (no childhood)\",\n  \"6\"   = \"6 — Identity + language + childhood (bilingual)\",\n  \"7\"   = \"7 — Identity + language + childhood (monolingual)\"\n)\n\ncel_colors <- c(\n  \"0\"   = \"#d9d9d9\",\n  \"1\"   = \"#fee0b6\",\n  \"1.5\" = \"#fdb863\",\n  \"2\"   = \"#e08214\",\n  \"3\"   = \"#b35806\",\n  \"4\"   = \"#cce5ff\",\n  \"4.5\" = \"#74add1\",\n  \"5\"   = \"#4393c3\",\n  \"6\"   = \"#2166ac\",\n  \"7\"   = \"#053061\"\n)"},"elapsedMs":59}</script></tool-result></div>

Now let me check that `albo_vars` is loaded in the session:

<div><tool-call id="toolu_016CDgRwUnXPnTDn3DBqwNCP"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\n# Check if albo_vars is available\nif (exists(\"albo_vars\")) {\n  dim(albo_vars)\n} else {\n  \"not loaded\"\n}\n","intent":"Check if albo_vars is in session"},"intent":"Check if albo_vars is in session","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\n# Check if albo_vars is available\nif (exists(\"albo_vars\")) {\n  dim(albo_vars)\n} else {\n  \"not loaded\"\n}\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01HCugP63TZM6KGx8ESkhAcn"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\n[1] 11365333       10\n [1] \"idep\"    \"iprov\"   \"imun\"    \"urbrur\"  \"albo_q1\" \"albo...[truncated, total 164 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[1] 11365333       10\n [1] \"idep\"    \"iprov\"   \"imun\"    \"urbrur\"  \"albo_q1\" \"albo_q2\" \"albo_q3\" \"albo_c\" \n [9] \"cel\"     \"cel_q2\""},"elapsedMs":2106}</script></tool-result></div>

Good — `albo_vars` has `urbrur` and `cel`. I also need `p26_edad` for the age version later. `albo_vars` doesn't have age, so I'll need to get that from the person file. But for now, let me build the urban/rural diverging chart first.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_015QYug3Xi9PxSRz3iWv1nui"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\nlibrary(tidyverse)\nlibrary(ggrepel)\n\n# Set up labels and colors (from the qmd)\ncel_labels...[truncated, total 1586 chars]","intent":"Build diverging bar data for CEL by urban/rural"},"intent":"Build diverging bar data for CEL by urban/rural","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\nlibrary(tidyverse)\nlibrary(ggrepel)\n\n# Set up labels and colors (from the qmd)\ncel_labels <- c(\n  \"0\"   = \"0 — No identity, no language\",\n  \"1\"   = \"1 — No identity, speaks (no childhood)\",\n  \"1.5\" = \"1.5 — No identity, childhood language only\",\n  \"2\"   = \"2 — No identity, speaks + childhood (bilingual)\",\n  \"3\"   = \"3 — No identity, speaks + childhood (monolingual)\",\n  \"4\"   = \"4 — Identity only\",\n  \"4.5\" = \"4.5 — Identity + childhood (no current language)\",\n  \"5\"   = \"5 — Identity + language (no childhood)\",\n  \"6\"   = \"6 — Identity + language + childhood (bilingual)\",\n  \"7\"   = \"7 — Identity + language + childhood (monolingual)\"\n)\n\ncel_colors <- c(\n  \"0\"   = \"#d9d9d9\",\n  \"1\"   = \"#fee0b6\",\n  \"1.5\" = \"#fdb863\",\n  \"2\"   = \"#e08214\",\n  \"3\"   = \"#b35806\",\n  \"4\"   = \"#cce5ff\",\n  \"4.5\" = \"#74add1\",\n  \"5\"   = \"#4393c3\",\n  \"6\"   = \"#2166ac\",\n  \"7\"   = \"#053061\"\n)\n\nurbrur_labels <- c(\"1\" = \"Urban\", \"2\" = \"Rural\")\n\n# Left side (no identity): 0, 1, 1.5, 2, 3\n# Right side (identity): 4, 4.5, 5, 6, 7\nleft_levels  <- c(\"0\", \"1\", \"1.5\", \"2\", \"3\")\nright_levels <- c(\"4\", \"4.5\", \"5\", \"6\", \"7\")\n\n# Aggregate by urban/rural × CEL\ncel_urbrur <- albo_vars |>\n  mutate(urbrur_label = urbrur_labels[as.character(urbrur)]) |>\n  filter(!is.na(urbrur_label), !is.na(cel)) |>\n  count(urbrur_label, cel) |>\n  mutate(cel_chr = as.character(cel))\n\n# Add side and n_plot\ncel_div <- cel_urbrur |>\n  mutate(\n    side   = if_else(cel_chr %in% left_levels, \"left\", \"right\"),\n    n_plot = if_else(side == \"left\", -n, n)\n  ) |>\n  group_by(urbrur_label) |>\n  mutate(total = sum(n)) |>\n  ungroup()\n\ncel_div\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_015QYug3Xi9PxSRz3iWv1nui"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\n# A tibble: 20 × 7\n   urbrur_label   cel       n cel_chr side    n_plot   total\n  ...[truncated, total 1393 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# A tibble: 20 × 7\n   urbrur_label   cel       n cel_chr side    n_plot   total\n   <chr>        <dbl>   <int> <chr>   <chr>    <int>   <int>\n 1 Rural          0    845945 0       left   -845945 3518625\n 2 Rural          1    105132 1       left   -105132 3518625\n 3 Rural          1.5   85947 1.5     left    -85947 3518625\n 4 Rural          2    118411 2       left   -118411 3518625\n 5 Rural          3     31429 3       left    -31429 3518625\n 6 Rural          4    540436 4       right   540436 3518625\n 7 Rural          4.5   32997 4.5     right    32997 3518625\n 8 Rural          5    481558 5       right   481558 3518625\n 9 Rural          6   1003432 6       right  1003432 3518625\n10 Rural          7    273338 7       right   273338 3518625\n11 Urban          0   5185878 0       left  -5185878 7846708\n12 Urban          1    423220 1       left   -423220 7846708\n13 Urban          1.5   40035 1.5     left    -40035 7846708\n14 Urban          2    213607 2       left   -213607 7846708\n15 Urban          3     13245 3       left    -13245 7846708\n16 Urban          4    756969 4       right   756969 7846708\n17 Urban          4.5   26165 4.5     right    26165 7846708\n18 Urban          5    610446 5       right   610446 7846708\n19 Urban          6    535691 6       right   535691 7846708\n20 Urban          7     41452 7       right    41452 7846708"},"elapsedMs":3572}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_017xfkxt9FFaAV4Rro9KMgzV"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\nlabel_compact <- function(x) {\n  case_when(\n    abs(x) >= 1e6 ~ paste0(round(abs(x) / 1e6...[truncated, total 4714 chars]","intent":"Create diverging bar chart of CEL by urban/rural"},"intent":"Create diverging bar chart of CEL by urban/rural","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\nlabel_compact <- function(x) {\n  case_when(\n    abs(x) >= 1e6 ~ paste0(round(abs(x) / 1e6, 1), \"M\"),\n    abs(x) >= 1e3 ~ paste0(round(abs(x) / 1e3, 1), \"K\"),\n    TRUE           ~ as.character(abs(x))\n  )\n}\n\n# Factor ordering: right levels first (inner→outer from axis), then left (inner→outer)\n# Left: 0 farthest out, 3 closest to axis\n# Right: 4 closest to axis, 7 farthest out\ncel_factor_order <- c(rev(right_levels), left_levels)\n\ncel_div <- cel_div |>\n  mutate(\n    cel_chr = factor(cel_chr, levels = cel_factor_order),\n    urbrur_label = factor(urbrur_label, levels = c(\"Urban\", \"Rural\"))\n  )\n\n# Compute segment label positions\nseg_labs_cel <- cel_div |>\n  arrange(urbrur_label, side, cel_chr) |>\n  group_by(urbrur_label, side) |>\n  mutate(\n    abs_cum   = cumsum(n),\n    abs_start = abs_cum - n,\n    mid       = if_else(side == \"left\",\n                        -(abs_start + abs_cum) / 2,\n                         (abs_start + abs_cum) / 2)\n  ) |>\n  ungroup() |>\n  filter(n > 15000) |>\n  mutate(\n    label    = label_compact(n),\n    text_col = if_else(\n      cel_chr %in% c(\"0\", \"6\", \"7\", \"3\", \"5\"),\n      \"white\", \"grey20\"\n    )\n  )\n\nx_range_cel <- max(abs(cel_div$n_plot))\nseg_inside_cel <- seg_labs_cel |> filter(n > x_range_cel * 0.04)\nseg_above_cel  <- seg_labs_cel |> filter(n <= x_range_cel * 0.04)\n\n# Right-side totals (identity side)\nright_totals_cel <- cel_div |>\n  filter(side == \"right\") |>\n  group_by(urbrur_label) |>\n  summarise(right_total = sum(n), .groups = \"drop\")\n\n# Y-axis labels with totals\ny_labs_cel <- cel_div |>\n  group_by(urbrur_label) |>\n  summarise(total = first(total), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  mutate(y_lab = paste0(urbrur_label, \"\\nn = \", scales::comma(total))) |>\n  pull(y_lab, name = urbrur_label)\n\n# Remap factor levels\ncel_div <- cel_div |>\n  mutate(urbrur_label = factor(y_labs_cel[as.character(urbrur_label)],\n                                levels = y_labs_cel))\nseg_inside_cel <- seg_inside_cel |>\n  mutate(urbrur_label = factor(y_labs_cel[as.character(urbrur_label)],\n                                levels = y_labs_cel))\nseg_above_cel <- seg_above_cel |>\n  mutate(urbrur_label = factor(y_labs_cel[as.character(urbrur_label)],\n                                levels = y_labs_cel))\nright_totals_cel <- right_totals_cel |>\n  mutate(urbrur_label = factor(y_labs_cel[as.character(urbrur_label)],\n                                levels = y_labs_cel))\n\nbs <- 14\n\nggplot(cel_div, aes(x = n_plot, y = urbrur_label, fill = cel_chr)) +\n  geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = FALSE) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = seg_inside_cel,\n    aes(x = mid, y = urbrur_label, label = label, color = text_col),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.26, fontface = \"bold\"\n  ) +\n  scale_color_identity() +\n  geom_text_repel(\n    data = seg_above_cel,\n    aes(x = mid, y = urbrur_label, label = label),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.21, fontface = \"bold\",\n    color = \"grey30\",\n    direction = \"x\", nudge_y = 0.44,\n    segment.size = 0.2, segment.color = \"grey60\",\n    min.segment.length = 0,\n    box.padding = 0.15, force = 3, max.overlaps = 20\n  ) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = right_totals_cel,\n    aes(x = right_total * 1.02, y = urbrur_label,\n        label = scales::comma(right_total)),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 0, size = bs * 0.26, fontface = \"bold\",\n    color = \"grey30\"\n  ) +\n  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1.2, color = \"grey20\") +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = -x_range_cel * 0.45, y = 2.55,\n           label = \"\\u2190  No indigenous identity (CEL 0\\u20133)\",\n           size = bs * 0.26, fontface = \"bold\", color = \"#4a5568\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = x_range_cel * 0.35, y = 2.55,\n           label = \"Indigenous identity (CEL 4\\u20137)  \\u2192\",\n           size = bs * 0.26, fontface = \"bold\", color = \"#2166ac\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  scale_x_continuous(\n    labels = function(x) scales::comma(abs(x)),\n    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.08, 0.14))\n  ) +\n  scale_fill_manual(values = cel_colors) +\n  coord_cartesian(clip = \"off\", ylim = c(0.4, 2.5)) +\n  labs(\n    title    = \"CEL distribution by urban/rural \\u2014 CNPV 2024\",\n    subtitle = \"Condici\\u00f3n \\u00c9tnico-Ling\\u00fc\\u00edstica (Alb\\u00f3 & Romero)  |  n = 11,365,333\",\n    x = NULL, y = NULL\n  ) +\n  theme_void(base_size = bs) +\n  theme(\n    plot.title    = element_text(face = \"bold\", size = rel(1.35), margin = margin(b = 3)),\n    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = \"grey40\", size = rel(0.8), margin = margin(b = 16)),\n    plot.margin   = margin(20, 30, 10, 10),\n    axis.text.y   = element_text(hjust = 1, color = \"grey20\",\n                                 size = rel(0.85), margin = margin(r = 8)),\n    axis.text.x   = element_text(color = \"grey50\", size = rel(0.6), margin = margin(t = 5))\n  )\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<tool-image call-id="toolu_017xfkxt9FFaAV4Rro9KMgzV" src="data:image/png;base64," width="672" height="672"></tool-image>



<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_017xfkxt9FFaAV4Rro9KMgzV"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Execution time: 687ms"},{"type":"image-data","data":"iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAqAAAAKgCAYAAABEPM/FAAAEDmlDQ1BrQ0dDb2xvclNwYWNlR2VuZXJpY1JHQgAAOI...[truncated, total 74280 chars]","mediaType":"image/png"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":""},"elapsedMs":788}</script></tool-result></div>

Good start — the structure is right but labels are colliding on the smaller segments. Let me refine the thresholds and label placement.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01Eqi5piHUUjBHV7VFe6mif4"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\n# Recalculate with better thresholds\nseg_labs_cel <- cel_div |>\n  arrange(urbrur_label, s...[truncated, total 3100 chars]","intent":"Refine label thresholds to reduce collision"},"intent":"Refine label thresholds to reduce collision","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\n# Recalculate with better thresholds\nseg_labs_cel <- cel_div |>\n  arrange(urbrur_label, side, cel_chr) |>\n  group_by(urbrur_label, side) |>\n  mutate(\n    abs_cum   = cumsum(n),\n    abs_start = abs_cum - n,\n    mid       = if_else(side == \"left\",\n                        -(abs_start + abs_cum) / 2,\n                         (abs_start + abs_cum) / 2)\n  ) |>\n  ungroup() |>\n  mutate(\n    label    = label_compact(n),\n    text_col = if_else(\n      cel_chr %in% c(\"0\", \"6\", \"7\", \"3\", \"5\"),\n      \"white\", \"grey20\"\n    )\n  )\n\n# Inside: only segments wide enough to fit text (~5% of x range per row)\n# Use per-row range for the threshold\nseg_inside_cel <- seg_labs_cel |> filter(n > x_range_cel * 0.06)\nseg_above_cel  <- seg_labs_cel |> filter(n <= x_range_cel * 0.06, n > x_range_cel * 0.005)\n\nggplot(cel_div, aes(x = n_plot, y = urbrur_label, fill = cel_chr)) +\n  geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = FALSE) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = seg_inside_cel,\n    aes(x = mid, y = urbrur_label, label = label, color = text_col),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.24, fontface = \"bold\"\n  ) +\n  scale_color_identity() +\n  geom_text_repel(\n    data = seg_above_cel,\n    aes(x = mid, y = urbrur_label, label = label),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.19, fontface = \"bold\",\n    color = \"grey30\",\n    direction = \"x\", nudge_y = 0.42,\n    segment.size = 0.2, segment.color = \"grey60\",\n    min.segment.length = 0,\n    box.padding = 0.1, force = 4, max.overlaps = 20\n  ) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = right_totals_cel,\n    aes(x = right_total * 1.02, y = urbrur_label,\n        label = scales::comma(right_total)),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 0, size = bs * 0.24, fontface = \"bold\",\n    color = \"grey30\"\n  ) +\n  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1.2, color = \"grey20\") +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = -x_range_cel * 0.45, y = 2.6,\n           label = \"\\u2190  No indigenous identity (CEL 0\\u20133)\",\n           size = bs * 0.24, fontface = \"bold\", color = \"#4a5568\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = x_range_cel * 0.35, y = 2.6,\n           label = \"Indigenous identity (CEL 4\\u20137)  \\u2192\",\n           size = bs * 0.24, fontface = \"bold\", color = \"#2166ac\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  scale_x_continuous(\n    labels = function(x) scales::comma(abs(x)),\n    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.06, 0.14))\n  ) +\n  scale_fill_manual(values = cel_colors) +\n  coord_cartesian(clip = \"off\", ylim = c(0.4, 2.5)) +\n  labs(\n    title    = \"CEL distribution by urban/rural \\u2014 CNPV 2024\",\n    subtitle = \"Condici\\u00f3n \\u00c9tnico-Ling\\u00fc\\u00edstica (Alb\\u00f3 & Romero)  |  n = 11,365,333\",\n    x = NULL, y = NULL\n  ) +\n  theme_void(base_size = bs) +\n  theme(\n    plot.title    = element_text(face = \"bold\", size = rel(1.35), margin = margin(b = 3)),\n    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = \"grey40\", size = rel(0.8), margin = margin(b = 16)),\n    plot.margin   = margin(20, 30, 10, 10),\n    axis.text.y   = element_text(hjust = 1, color = \"grey20\",\n                                 size = rel(0.85), margin = margin(r = 8)),\n    axis.text.x   = element_text(color = \"grey50\", size = rel(0.6), margin = margin(t = 5))\n  )\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

Better, but there's still some label collision on the right side, especially where segments are narrow. Let me also add a legend since CEL levels aren't self-evident from color alone, and clean up the overlap.

The labels with CEL numbers are helpful but still colliding on the right side where segments are narrow. The core issue is that Urban has a massive CEL 0 bar dominating the left, making the right-side identity segments very compressed. Let me try a wider figure and slightly reduce text sizes for the tight areas.

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_0168jGGZmeSU8XYZeCSKc5oi"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Execution time: 596ms"},{"type":"image-data","data":"iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAABUAAAAHgCAYAAABkVcfGAAAEDmlDQ1BrQ0dDb2xvclNwYWNlR2VuZXJpY1JHQgAAOI...[truncated, total 81492 chars]","mediaType":"image/png"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":""},"elapsedMs":681}</script></tool-result></div>

That's much cleaner. The Rural label for "4: 540.4K" is slightly clipped at the right edge. Let me check if you're happy with this overall structure, or if there are things to adjust. Here's a summary of what you see:

- **Left side** (CEL 0–3): people without indigenous self-identification, ordered from CEL 0 at the far left to CEL 3 nearest the axis
- **Right side** (CEL 4–7): people with indigenous identity, CEL 4 nearest the axis to CEL 7 farthest right
- Each row shows counts with labels (CEL level: count), and the total self-identifiers (right-side sum) at the end of the bar

The urban/rural contrast is stark — Rural has 2.33M identifiers out of 3.5M (66%), while Urban has 1.97M out of 7.8M (25%). The Rural population also skews much more toward higher CEL levels (6, 7) while Urban is dominated by the massive CEL 0 block.

Does this look right as a first draft? Once you approve I'll replicate it for age strata.

I'll suggest some directions below.


<span class="suggestion">Adjust colors, label sizes, or thresholds before finalizing.</span>


<span class="suggestion">Approve this and proceed with the age-stratified version (0-14, 15-29, 30-44, 45-59, 60+).</span>


<span class="suggestion">Add a legend strip below the plot showing CEL level descriptions.</span>





## User

The labels don't point to the midpoints on the left, but rather assume that 1, 1.5, and 2 are to the left of the zero block. (The current block order is correct, just the labels are wrong.) Add a color legend below the main graph. 

Proceed to generate an age-stratified plot as well.

The user wants two fixes:
1. Fix the label midpoint calculation - the issue is that the cumsum for left-side labels is wrong. The stacking order in ggplot for the left side has 0 farthest left, then 1, 1.5, 2, 3 closest to axis. But my cumsum is computing positions assuming a different order. Let me think about this.

The factor order is `c("7", "6", "5", "4.5", "4", "0", "1", "1.5", "2", "3")` - reversed right levels first, then left levels. For ggplot stacked bars, the last level in the factor is drawn closest to the axis.


For the left side, the levels in factor order are "0", "1", "1.5", "2", "3". The last level "3" is closest to axis, "0" is farthest. So ggplot stacks them: 3 closest to 0, then 2, then 1.5, then 1, then 0 farthest out. But in my cumsum calculation, I'm arranging by `side, cel_chr` and cel_chr is a factor with order `c("7", "6", "5", "4.5", "4", "0", "1", "1.5", "2", "3")`, so I need to check if the arrangement is actually matching how ggplot stacks the bars.

For the left side, the cel_chr values are "0", "1", "1.5", "2", "3" in factor order positions 6 through 10. When I arrange by cel_chr, I get them in that order, but ggplot stacks horizontal bars in reverse factor order—meaning "3" sits closest to the axis and "0" farthest out. So my cumsum needs to start from "3" and work outward, which means I should arrange in descending factor order instead. I need to reconsider how ggplot2 stacks bars with the fill aesthetic. The factor level order determines stacking position—the first level sits closest to zero, and the last level extends farthest out. Given my factor order, I'm realizing that "4" should be closest to the axis on the right side, not "7", which suggests I may need to reverse the factor levels to get the desired stacking order. I'm realizing the stacking order for negative values works differently—the first factor level in that direction starts at zero and extends outward, so I need to trace through which CEL category actually appears closest to the axis on the left side of the plot. I'm realizing the stacking behavior is consistent for both positive and negative values—the first factor level is always closest to the axis, and subsequent levels stack further away. So if the blocks are currently ordered correctly with 0 farthest left, the factor levels must already be in the right sequence, meaning I just need to fix the labels to match. I see the issue now—the "0" category is the largest block at 845.9K and sits closest to the axis, while the smaller categories (1, 1.5, 2, 3) with values around 31-118K are stacked beyond it on the far left. The user expects "0" to be farthest left, but based on the stacking order, it should be closest to the axis instead.

Wait, let me reconsider what the user is saying. They're saying the block order itself is correct, but the label positions are wrong—my cumsum calculation for placing the labels assumed the opposite stacking order, so the labels are pointing to the wrong midpoints.

Examining the cumsum code...

Calculating label positions... I'm realizing I might be overcomplicating this—let me actually look at what the user is seeing on their chart. The label "0: 845.9K" is pointing to the block closest to the axis, and if that's genuinely the zero category in grey, then the label placement is accurate. But the user is saying the labels suggest 1, 1.5, and 2 are positioned to the left of the zero block, which would mean my stacking order assumption is backwards from what ggplot is actually rendering.

Let me just extract the actual bar positions from the rendered plot using ggplot_build to see exactly where each factor level ends up, rather than trying to reason through the stacking logic. at mid=-1171K (farthest). This matches the stacking.

So why does the user say the labels are wrong? Maybe the issue is different - perhaps the arrange order is wrong and the cumsum doesn't match the ggplot stacking.

Let me check the arrange: `arrange(urbrur_label, side, cel_chr)`. The `cel_chr` is a factor with levels in order "7", "6", "5", "4.5", "4", "0", "1", "1.5", "2", "3". For left side items (0, 1, 1.5, 2, 3), they appear at positions 6-10 in that factor. So the arrange gives: 0, 1, 1.5, 2, 3 - the same order as ggplot stacking - which means my cumsum should be matching correctly.

Wait, actually I need to reconsider how `position_stack` works. It stacks in reverse order of the factor levels - the last level sits closest to zero and the first level goes farthest out. So for the left side, 3 would be closest to the axis and 0 would be farthest, which matches the user saying "0 on the far left." But my cumsum starts with 0 closest to the axis, which is backwards. I need to reverse the arrange order for the cumsum calculation to fix this.

For the right side with levels 7, 6, 5, 4.5, 4, the stacking would put 4 closest to the axis and 7 farthest right - and the user hasn't complained about that side, so it seems correct. Let me check the actual ggplot2 behavior: `position_stack` with `reverse = FALSE` (the default for `geom_col`) stacks groups in order of factor levels with the first level at the bottom, which for horizontal bars means closest to the axis.

Rather than keep second-guessing the stacking order, I should just extract the actual positions from `ggplot_build()` and use those for the labels - that way I'm guaranteed to be correct. <div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01J5EQyGXFgtCmPLFcfB4w4e"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"Todo List Updated (3 items):\n→ Fix label midpoint calculation using ggplot_build to get ac...[truncated, total 243 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"Todo List Updated (3 items):\n→ Fix label midpoint calculation using ggplot_build to get actual bar positions (in progress)\n○ Add color legend below the plot (pending)\n○ Generate age-stratified version (0-14, 15-29, 30-44, 45-59, 60+) (pending)"},"elapsedMs":2}</script></tool-result></div>

The cumsum-based label positions don't match ggplot's actual stacking order for negative bars. Let me extract the real positions via `ggplot_build()`.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01XvTXRMa5jrJy71GrUjJ4jJ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\n# Build a bare plot to extract actual bar positions\np_bare <- ggplot(cel_div, aes(x = n_p...[truncated, total 664 chars]","intent":"Extract actual bar positions from ggplot_build"},"intent":"Extract actual bar positions from ggplot_build","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\n# Build a bare plot to extract actual bar positions\np_bare <- ggplot(cel_div, aes(x = n_plot, y = urbrur_label, fill = cel_chr)) +\n  geom_col(width = 0.65) +\n  scale_fill_manual(values = cel_colors)\n\nbd <- ggplot_build(p_bare)\nbar_positions <- bd$data[[1]] |>\n  as_tibble() |>\n  select(fill, x, xmin, xmax, y, group) |>\n  mutate(\n    mid = (xmin + xmax) / 2,\n    width = abs(xmax - xmin)\n  )\n\n# Map back to our data — match by y (row position) and fill color\n# Need to map fill hex back to cel_chr\ncolor_to_cel <- setNames(names(cel_colors), cel_colors)\n\nbar_positions <- bar_positions |>\n  mutate(cel_chr = color_to_cel[fill])\n\nbar_positions |> arrange(y, xmin)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01XvTXRMa5jrJy71GrUjJ4jJ"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\n# A tibble: 20 × 9\n   fill          x     xmin    xmax y          group       mid ...[truncated, total 1788 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# A tibble: 20 × 9\n   fill          x     xmin    xmax y          group       mid   width cel_chr\n   <chr>     <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl> <mppd_dsc> <int>     <dbl>   <dbl> <chr>  \n 1 #d9d9d9 -690107 -5875985 -690107 1              6 -3283046  5185878 0      \n 2 #fee0b6 -266887  -690107 -266887 1              7  -478497   423220 1      \n 3 #fdb863 -226852  -266887 -226852 1              8  -246870.   40035 1.5    \n 4 #e08214  -13245  -226852  -13245 1              9  -120048.  213607 2      \n 5 #b35806       0   -13245       0 1             10    -6622.   13245 3      \n 6 #cce5ff  756969        0  756969 1              5   378484.  756969 4      \n 7 #74add1  783134   756969  783134 1              4   770052.   26165 4.5    \n 8 #4393c3 1393580   783134 1393580 1              3  1088357   610446 5      \n 9 #2166ac 1929271  1393580 1929271 1              2  1661426.  535691 6      \n10 #053061 1970723  1929271 1970723 1              1  1949997    41452 7      \n11 #d9d9d9 -340919 -1186864 -340919 2             16  -763892.  845945 0      \n12 #fee0b6 -235787  -340919 -235787 2             17  -288353   105132 1      \n13 #fdb863 -149840  -235787 -149840 2             18  -192814.   85947 1.5    \n14 #e08214  -31429  -149840  -31429 2             19   -90634.  118411 2      \n15 #b35806       0   -31429       0 2             20   -15714.   31429 3      \n16 #cce5ff  540436        0  540436 2             15   270218   540436 4      \n17 #74add1  573433   540436  573433 2             14   556934.   32997 4.5    \n18 #4393c3 1054991   573433 1054991 2             13   814212   481558 5      \n19 #2166ac 2058423  1054991 2058423 2             12  1556707  1003432 6      \n20 #053061 2331761  2058423 2331761 2             11  2195092   273338 7"},"elapsedMs":342}</script></tool-result></div>

Now I can see the actual positions. The stacking puts 0 farthest left and 3 closest to axis — confirming the user's description. Now let me rebuild using these real midpoints.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_019mXkLnjZKu1bst47kdCWi4"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\n# Join actual midpoints back to our data\n# y=1 is Urban, y=2 is Rural (factor order)\ny_to...[truncated, total 3553 chars]","intent":"Rebuild plot with correct label midpoints from ggplot_build and color legend"},"intent":"Rebuild plot with correct label midpoints from ggplot_build and color legend","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\n# Join actual midpoints back to our data\n# y=1 is Urban, y=2 is Rural (factor order)\ny_to_label <- c(\"1\" = levels(cel_div$urbrur_label)[1],\n                 \"2\" = levels(cel_div$urbrur_label)[2])\n\nseg_data <- bar_positions |>\n  mutate(\n    urbrur_label = factor(y_to_label[as.character(as.integer(y))],\n                          levels = levels(cel_div$urbrur_label)),\n    n = as.integer(width),\n    side = if_else(xmax <= 0, \"left\", \"right\"),\n    text_col = if_else(\n      cel_chr %in% c(\"0\", \"6\", \"7\", \"3\", \"5\"),\n      \"white\", \"grey20\"\n    ),\n    label = paste0(cel_chr, \": \", label_compact(n))\n  )\n\n# Inside: wide enough to fit text\nseg_inside_final <- seg_data |> filter(width > x_range_cel * 0.06)\nseg_above_final  <- seg_data |> filter(width <= x_range_cel * 0.06,\n                                        width > x_range_cel * 0.005)\n\n# Right-side totals (already computed)\n# right_totals_cel is already correct\n\nggplot(cel_div, aes(x = n_plot, y = urbrur_label, fill = cel_chr)) +\n  geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = TRUE) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = seg_inside_final,\n    aes(x = mid, y = urbrur_label, label = label, color = text_col),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.22, fontface = \"bold\"\n  ) +\n  scale_color_identity() +\n  geom_text_repel(\n    data = seg_above_final,\n    aes(x = mid, y = urbrur_label, label = label),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.17,\n    color = \"grey30\",\n    direction = \"x\", nudge_y = 0.42,\n    segment.size = 0.2, segment.color = \"grey60\",\n    min.segment.length = 0,\n    box.padding = 0.08, force = 6, max.overlaps = 30,\n    seed = 7841\n  ) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = right_totals_cel,\n    aes(x = right_total * 1.02, y = urbrur_label,\n        label = scales::comma(right_total)),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 0, size = bs * 0.24, fontface = \"bold\",\n    color = \"grey30\"\n  ) +\n  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1.2, color = \"grey20\") +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = -x_range_cel * 0.5, y = 2.65,\n           label = \"\\u2190  No indigenous identity (CEL 0\\u20133)\",\n           size = bs * 0.22, fontface = \"bold\", color = \"#4a5568\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = x_range_cel * 0.3, y = 2.65,\n           label = \"Indigenous identity (CEL 4\\u20137)  \\u2192\",\n           size = bs * 0.22, fontface = \"bold\", color = \"#2166ac\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  scale_x_continuous(\n    labels = function(x) scales::comma(abs(x)),\n    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.06, 0.14))\n  ) +\n  scale_fill_manual(\n    values = cel_colors,\n    labels = cel_labels,\n    name = NULL,\n    guide = guide_legend(nrow = 2, override.aes = list(size = 4))\n  ) +\n  coord_cartesian(clip = \"off\", ylim = c(0.4, 2.55)) +\n  labs(\n    title    = \"CEL distribution by urban/rural \\u2014 CNPV 2024\",\n    subtitle = \"Condici\\u00f3n \\u00c9tnico-Ling\\u00fc\\u00edstica (Alb\\u00f3 & Romero)  |  n = 11,365,333\",\n    x = NULL, y = NULL\n  ) +\n  theme_void(base_size = bs) +\n  theme(\n    plot.title    = element_text(face = \"bold\", size = rel(1.35), margin = margin(b = 3)),\n    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = \"grey40\", size = rel(0.8), margin = margin(b = 16)),\n    plot.margin   = margin(20, 30, 10, 10),\n    axis.text.y   = element_text(hjust = 1, color = \"grey20\",\n                                 size = rel(0.85), margin = margin(r = 8)),\n    axis.text.x   = element_text(color = \"grey50\", size = rel(0.6), margin = margin(t = 5)),\n    legend.position = \"bottom\",\n    legend.text     = element_text(size = rel(0.55)),\n    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, \"cm\"),\n    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.15, \"cm\"),\n    legend.margin = margin(t = 10)\n  )\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<tool-image call-id="toolu_019mXkLnjZKu1bst47kdCWi4" src="data:image/png;base64," width="1344" height="480"></tool-image>



<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_019mXkLnjZKu1bst47kdCWi4"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Execution time: 941ms"},{"type":"image-data","data":"iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAABUAAAAHgCAYAAABkVcfGAAAEDmlDQ1BrQ0dDb2xvclNwYWNlR2VuZXJpY1JHQgAAOI...[truncated, total 102164 chars]","mediaType":"image/png"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":""},"elapsedMs":1060}</script></tool-result></div>

Labels now point to the correct midpoints. Let me tidy up the legend ordering (currently reversed) and proceed to the age-stratified version.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01QqrgzBbYBTgqXUhQcna5wS"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"todoWrite","input":{"todos":[{"content":"Fix label midpoint calculation using ggplot_build to get actual bar positions","status":"completed"},{"content":"Add color legend below the plot","status":"completed"},{"content":"Generate age-stratified version (0-14, 15-29, 30-44, 45-59, 60+)","status":"in_progress"}]},"caption":"2 done, 1 active"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01QqrgzBbYBTgqXUhQcna5wS"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"Todo List Updated (3 items):\n✓ Fix label midpoint calculation using ggplot_build to get ac...[truncated, total 247 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"Todo List Updated (3 items):\n✓ Fix label midpoint calculation using ggplot_build to get actual bar positions (completed)\n✓ Add color legend below the plot (completed)\n→ Generate age-stratified version (0-14, 15-29, 30-44, 45-59, 60+) (in progress)"},"elapsedMs":1}</script></tool-result></div>

Now I need age data. `albo_vars` doesn't have `p26_edad`, so I'll need to pull it from the person file.

<div><tool-call id="toolu_01La56xNr1DRq5wWepYQCRnV"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\n# Need age from the person file — use arrow lazy scan\nlibrary(arrow)\n\nds_persona <- open_...[truncated, total 787 chars]","intent":"Get age from person file and compute CEL × age_group counts","timeout":120000},"intent":"Get age from person file and compute CEL × age_group counts","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\n# Need age from the person file — use arrow lazy scan\nlibrary(arrow)\n\nds_persona <- open_dataset(\n  file.path(DATA_DIR, \"Persona_CPV-2024.csv\"),\n  format = \"csv\", delimiter = \";\"\n)\n\n# Just need idep, iprov, imun, p26_edad to join with albo_vars\n# But albo_vars has 11.4M rows — no row key. Both are in the same order.\n# Simpler: compute CEL × age_group directly\n\n# albo_vars rows are in same order as persona file, so we can cbind age\nage_col <- ds_persona |>\n  select(p26_edad) |>\n  collect()\n\nalbo_vars$age <- age_col$p26_edad\n\nalbo_vars |>\n  mutate(age_group = case_when(\n    age <  15 ~ \"0-14\",\n    age <  30 ~ \"15-29\",\n    age <  45 ~ \"30-44\",\n    age <  60 ~ \"45-59\",\n    TRUE      ~ \"60+\"\n  )) |>\n  filter(!is.na(cel), !is.na(age_group)) |>\n  count(age_group, cel) |>\n  head(15)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01La56xNr1DRq5wWepYQCRnV"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\n# A tibble: 15 × 3\n   age_group   cel       n\n   <chr>     <dbl>   <int>\n 1 0-14  ...[truncated, total 511 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# A tibble: 15 × 3\n   age_group   cel       n\n   <chr>     <dbl>   <int>\n 1 0-14        0   1612234\n 2 0-14        1    137949\n 3 0-14        1.5   35110\n 4 0-14        2     87114\n 5 0-14        3     12013\n 6 0-14        4    358344\n 7 0-14        4.5   16724\n 8 0-14        5    297150\n 9 0-14        6    419779\n10 0-14        7     87427\n11 15-29       0   1608586\n12 15-29       1    144746\n13 15-29       1.5   32594\n14 15-29       2     90162\n15 15-29       3     11891"},"elapsedMs":10536}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_01QkJYxKNjwG2kmVFaVamM5d"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\n# Build the age-stratified diverging data\nage_order <- c(\"0-14\", \"15-29\", \"30-44\", \"45-59...[truncated, total 2450 chars]","intent":"Build age-stratified diverging data and extract bar positions"},"intent":"Build age-stratified diverging data and extract bar positions","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\n# Build the age-stratified diverging data\nage_order <- c(\"0-14\", \"15-29\", \"30-44\", \"45-59\", \"60+\")\n\ncel_age <- albo_vars |>\n  mutate(age_group = case_when(\n    age <  15 ~ \"0-14\",\n    age <  30 ~ \"15-29\",\n    age <  45 ~ \"30-44\",\n    age <  60 ~ \"45-59\",\n    TRUE      ~ \"60+\"\n  )) |>\n  filter(!is.na(cel), !is.na(age_group)) |>\n  count(age_group, cel) |>\n  mutate(cel_chr = as.character(cel))\n\ncel_age_div <- cel_age |>\n  mutate(\n    side   = if_else(cel_chr %in% left_levels, \"left\", \"right\"),\n    n_plot = if_else(side == \"left\", -n, n),\n    cel_chr = factor(cel_chr, levels = cel_factor_order),\n    age_group = factor(age_group, levels = age_order)\n  ) |>\n  group_by(age_group) |>\n  mutate(total = sum(n)) |>\n  ungroup()\n\n# Y-axis labels with totals\ny_labs_age <- cel_age_div |>\n  group_by(age_group) |>\n  summarise(total = first(total), .groups = \"drop\") |>\n  mutate(y_lab = paste0(age_group, \"\\nn = \", scales::comma(total))) |>\n  pull(y_lab, name = age_group)\n\ncel_age_div <- cel_age_div |>\n  mutate(age_group = factor(y_labs_age[as.character(age_group)],\n                            levels = y_labs_age))\n\n# Right-side totals\nright_totals_age <- cel_age_div |>\n  filter(side == \"right\") |>\n  group_by(age_group) |>\n  summarise(right_total = sum(n), .groups = \"drop\")\n\n# Build bare plot to get actual positions\np_bare_age <- ggplot(cel_age_div, aes(x = n_plot, y = age_group, fill = cel_chr)) +\n  geom_col(width = 0.65) +\n  scale_fill_manual(values = cel_colors)\n\nbd_age <- ggplot_build(p_bare_age)\n\ncolor_to_cel <- setNames(names(cel_colors), cel_colors)\nage_y_levels <- levels(cel_age_div$age_group)\n\nbar_pos_age <- bd_age$data[[1]] |>\n  as_tibble() |>\n  select(fill, x, xmin, xmax, y, group) |>\n  mutate(\n    mid = (xmin + xmax) / 2,\n    width = abs(xmax - xmin),\n    cel_chr = color_to_cel[fill],\n    age_group = factor(age_y_levels[as.integer(y)], levels = age_y_levels),\n    n = as.integer(width),\n    side = if_else(xmax <= 0, \"left\", \"right\"),\n    text_col = if_else(\n      cel_chr %in% c(\"0\", \"6\", \"7\", \"3\", \"5\"),\n      \"white\", \"grey20\"\n    ),\n    label = paste0(cel_chr, \": \", label_compact(n))\n  )\n\nx_range_age <- max(abs(cel_age_div$n_plot))\nseg_inside_age <- bar_pos_age |> filter(width > x_range_age * 0.06)\nseg_above_age  <- bar_pos_age |> filter(width <= x_range_age * 0.06,\n                                         width > x_range_age * 0.005)\n\nseg_inside_age |> select(age_group, cel_chr, n, mid) |> arrange(age_group, mid)\n","language":"r"}}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01QkJYxKNjwG2kmVFaVamM5d"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"content","value":[{"type":"text","text":"Output:\n# A tibble: 23 × 4\n   age_group              cel_chr       n       mid\n   <fct>   ...[truncated, total 734 chars]"}]},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"# A tibble: 23 × 4\n   age_group              cel_chr       n       mid\n   <fct>                  <chr>     <int>     <dbl>\n 1 \"0-14\\nn = 3,063,844\"  0       1612234 -1078303 \n 2 \"0-14\\nn = 3,063,844\"  1        137949  -203212.\n 3 \"0-14\\nn = 3,063,844\"  4        358344   179172 \n 4 \"0-14\\nn = 3,063,844\"  5        297150   523643 \n 5 \"0-14\\nn = 3,063,844\"  6        419779   882108.\n 6 \"15-29\\nn = 3,034,152\" 0       1608586 -1083686 \n 7 \"15-29\\nn = 3,034,152\" 1        144746  -207020 \n 8 \"15-29\\nn = 3,034,152\" 4        342732   171366 \n 9 \"15-29\\nn = 3,034,152\" 5        293091   505020.\n10 \"15-29\\nn = 3,034,152\" 6        410871   857002.\n# ℹ 13 more rows\n# ℹ Use `print(n = ...)` to see more rows"},"elapsedMs":4070}</script></tool-result></div>



<div><tool-call id="toolu_01MMzctd5ENbMW7UkEZ4Q1q1"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"runCode","input":{"code":"\nggplot(cel_age_div, aes(x = n_plot, y = age_group, fill = cel_chr)) +\n  geom_col(width = ...[truncated, total 2621 chars]","intent":"Create age-stratified diverging CEL bar chart"},"intent":"Create age-stratified diverging CEL bar chart","inputPreview":{"type":"code","value":"\nggplot(cel_age_div, aes(x = n_plot, y = age_group, fill = cel_chr)) +\n  geom_col(width = 0.65, show.legend = TRUE) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = seg_inside_age,\n    aes(x = mid, y = age_group, label = label, color = text_col),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.20, fontface = \"bold\"\n  ) +\n  scale_color_identity() +\n  geom_text_repel(\n    data = seg_above_age,\n    aes(x = mid, y = age_group, label = label),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, size = bs * 0.15,\n    color = \"grey30\",\n    direction = \"x\", nudge_y = 0.42,\n    segment.size = 0.2, segment.color = \"grey60\",\n    min.segment.length = 0,\n    box.padding = 0.08, force = 6, max.overlaps = 30,\n    seed = 3291\n  ) +\n  geom_text(\n    data = right_totals_age,\n    aes(x = right_total * 1.02, y = age_group,\n        label = scales::comma(right_total)),\n    inherit.aes = FALSE, hjust = 0, size = bs * 0.22, fontface = \"bold\",\n    color = \"grey30\"\n  ) +\n  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 1.2, color = \"grey20\") +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = -x_range_age * 0.55, y = 5.65,\n           label = \"\\u2190  No indigenous identity (CEL 0\\u20133)\",\n           size = bs * 0.20, fontface = \"bold\", color = \"#4a5568\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = x_range_age * 0.35, y = 5.65,\n           label = \"Indigenous identity (CEL 4\\u20137)  \\u2192\",\n           size = bs * 0.20, fontface = \"bold\", color = \"#2166ac\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  scale_x_continuous(\n    labels = function(x) scales::comma(abs(x)),\n    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.06, 0.14))\n  ) +\n  scale_fill_manual(\n    values = cel_colors,\n    labels = cel_labels,\n    name = NULL,\n    guide = guide_legend(nrow = 2, override.aes = list(size = 4))\n  ) +\n  coord_cartesian(clip = \"off\", ylim = c(0.4, 5.55)) +\n  labs(\n    title    = \"CEL distribution by age group \\u2014 CNPV 2024\",\n    subtitle = \"Condici\\u00f3n \"bold\", size = rel(1.35), margin = margin(b = 3)),\n    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = \"grey40\", size = rel(0.8), margin = margin(b = 16)),\n    plot.margin   = margin(20, 30, 10, 10),\n rel(0.6), margin = margin(t = 5)),\n    legend.position = \"bottom\",\n    legend.text     = element_text(size = rel(0.55)),\n    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, \"cm\"),\n    legend.spacing.x = <div><tool-result call-id="toolu_0166CpKTFJiuP5iygEmH2jLF"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"Todo List Updated (3 items):\n✓ Fix label midpoint calculation using ggplot_build to get ac...[truncated, total 245 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"Todo List Updated (3 items):\n✓ Fix label midpoint calculation using ggplot_build to get actual bar positions (completed)\n✓ Add color legend below the plot (completed)\n✓ Generate age-stratified version (0-14, 15-29, 30-44, 45-59, 60+) (completed)"},"elapsedMs":0}</script></tool-result></div>

Both plots are done. The age-stratified version reveals a clear age gradient:

- The **right-side (identity) bars shrink with younger cohorts** — from 442K identifiers among 60+ (36% of that age group) to 1.18M among 0-14 (39%), though the share is actually slightly higher for children in absolute terms because the younger cohorts are much larger.
- The **composition shifts markedly**: among 60+, CEL 6 and 7 (full identity + language + childhood) dominate the right side, while younger cohorts have proportionally more CEL 4 (identity only, no language) — consistent with the language shift findings from the other Qmd.
- CEL 0 (no identity, no language) dominates the left side uniformly across all age groups.

The repel labels for the 60+ row are a bit tight on the right side given the smaller total. Let me know if you'd like adjustments to either plot before we write them into the Qmd.


<span class="suggestion">Refine label thresholds or text sizes for the 60+ row where segments are compressed.</span>


<span class="suggestion">Write both plots as new code chunks into albo-restudy-2024.qmd.</span>


<span class="suggestion">Add percentage annotations alongside the counts to show the share within each age group.</span>

= \"#4a5568\", hjust = 0.5) +\n  annotate(\"text\", x = x_range_cel * 0.3, y = 2.65,\n           label = \"Indigenous identity (CEL 4\\u20137)  \\u2192\",\n           size = bs * 0.22, fontface = \"grey40\", size = rel(0.8), margin = margin(b = 16)),\n    plot.margin   = margin(20, 30, 10, 10),\n    axis.text.y   = element_text(hjust = 1, color = \"grey20\",\n <div><tool-call id="toolu_01E64GbG43diAa7ucAS3w7Eu"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"name":"read","input":{"file_path":"/Users/bjorkjcr/Dropbox (Personal)/R/wiki-graph/albo-restudy-2024.qmd","offset":735,"limit":40},"caption":"albo-restudy-2024.qmd:736-775"}</script></tool-call></div>

<div><tool-result call-id="toolu_01E64GbG43diAa7ucAS3w7Eu"><script type="application/json">{"version":1,"status":"success","result":{"type":"text","value":"[File: 41.1KB, 1207 lines, showing lines 736-775 (truncated)]\n   736→  ) +\n   737→  theme_...[truncated, total 1696 chars]"},"resultPreview":{"type":"code","value":"[File: 41.1KB, 1207 lines, showing lines 736-775 (truncated)]\n  ) +\n  theme_minimal(base_size = 11)\n```\n\n#### 2001 vs. 2024 comparison\n\nThe 2001 distribution comes from Cuadro 8.1 (p. 190) of Molina Barrios & Albó\n(2006), based on the 2001 Census. The original Albó scale defines 8 integer\nlevels (0–7); the 2024 restudy adds half-steps 1.5 and 4.5 for trait\ncombinations not in the original scheme. For comparability, 2024 half-steps are\ncollapsed to their floor values (1.5 → 1, 4.5 → 4).\n\n```{r}\n#| label: cel-2001-vs-2024\n#| fig-height: 5.5\n\n# 2001 data from Molina Barrios & Albó (2006), Cuadro 8.1\nmolina_albo_8_1_en <- readRDS(\"data/molina_albo_8_1_en.rds\")\n\ncel_2001 <- molina_albo_8_1_en |>\n  transmute(cel = as.numeric(cel), pct = pop_total / sum(pop_total)) |>\n  mutate(year = \"2001\")\n\n# 2024 data with half-steps collapsed to floor\ncel_2024 <- albo_vars |>\n  mutate(cel = floor(cel)) |>\n  count(cel) |>\n  mutate(pct = n / sum(n), year = \"2024\") |>\n  select(cel, pct, year)\n\ncel_labels_int <- c(\n  \"0\" = \"0 — No identity, no language\",\n  \"1\" = \"1 — No identity, speaks (no childhood)\",\n  \"2\" = \"2 — No identity, speaks + childhood (bilingual)\",\n  \"3\" = \"3 — No identity, speaks + childhood (monolingual)\",\n  \"4\" = \"4 — Identity only\",\n  \"5\" = \"5 — Identity + language (no childhood)\",\n  \"6\" = \"6 — Identity + language + childhood (bilingual)\",\n  \"7\" = \"7 — Identity + language + childhood (monolingual)\"\n)"},"elapsedMs":78}</script></tool-result></div>
